channel.title: Opportunities for Youth
channel.description: Empowering Youth through Access to Information and Opportunities

item.title: Enlight Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Program for Youth Mental Wellbeing Leaders and Social Entrepreneurs
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Enlight Fellowship 2026, powered by Watson Institute and supported by the Enlight Foundation, is a prestigious fully funded program designed to empower emerging leaders working to improve youth mental wellbeing worldwide. This highly competitive fellowship equips participants with the entrepreneurial skills, leadership training, mentorship, and global network needed to scale impactful solutions. If you […]

The post Enlight Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Program for Youth Mental Wellbeing Leaders and Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Enlight Fellowship 2026, powered by Watson Institute and supported by the Enlight Foundation, is a prestigious fully funded program designed to empower emerging leaders working to improve youth mental wellbeing worldwide. This highly competitive fellowship equips participants with the entrepreneurial skills, leadership training, mentorship, and global network needed to scale impactful solutions.

If you are building a venture focused on mental health and social impact, this fellowship offers a transformative opportunity to accelerate your growth and expand your global influence.

About the Enlight Fellowship

The Enlight Fellowship is a 16-week leadership and entrepreneurship development program tailored specifically for individuals addressing youth mental wellbeing challenges. It combines immersive learning, expert mentorship, and practical application to help fellows scale their ventures and deepen their impact.

Core Objectives of the Fellowship

  • Strengthen leadership capacity among youth-focused innovators
  • Foster cross-cultural collaboration and global partnerships
  • Equip fellows with tools to scale sustainable mental wellbeing solutions
  • Build a long-term global network of changemakers

The program begins with a week-long immersive experience, followed by structured virtual sessions, mentorship, and hands-on project implementation.

Priority Application Deadline

  • Priority Deadline: July 5, 2026
  • Applications are reviewed on a rolling basis after this date until all slots are filled

Final decisions are announced by July 20, 2026, and the program officially begins on August 17, 2026.

Program Timeline and Key Dates

Program Duration

  • August 17 – December 18, 2026

In-Person Immersive Experience (Choose One Location)

  1. Boulder, Colorado
    • Week 1: August 17 – August 21, 2026
    • Week 2: August 24 – August 27, 2026 (Virtual continuation)
  2. Qingshan Village, Hangzhou, China
    • Week 1: July 3 – July 9, 2026
    • Week 2: August 24 – August 27, 2026 (Virtual continuation)

Additional Components

  • Regional networking events (dates to be announced)
  • Final Summit: December 18, 2026

What the Enlight Fellowship Offers

1. Leadership and Entrepreneurship Training

Participants engage in hands-on workshops covering:

  • Business development and strategy
  • Financial literacy and venture sustainability
  • Entrepreneurial leadership and innovation

2. Financial Tools and Resources

  • Access to practical frameworks for managing and scaling ventures
  • Guidance from financial and business experts
  • Support tailored to early-stage social enterprises

3. Basecamps: Community Impact Initiatives

Fellows will:

  • Design and lead a one-day workshop in their local community
  • Receive training and a stipend to execute their Basecamp
  • Strengthen local entrepreneurial ecosystems

4. Pitch Training and Global Exposure

  • Develop a compelling pitch for your venture
  • Present at a global Summit to:
    • Investors
    • Mentors
    • Community leaders

5. Mentorship and Expert Guidance

Fellows learn directly from experienced professionals, including:

  • Entrepreneurs
  • Industry experts
  • Leadership coaches

6. Global Network and Alumni Support

  • Join a lifelong community of changemakers
  • Access ongoing mentorship and collaboration opportunities

Weekly Commitment and Structure

Participants should be prepared for an engaging and structured schedule:

  • Weekly Workshops: Held Tuesday to Thursday
  • Two available time slots:
    • 10:00 AM – 12:00 PM MT
    • 6:00 PM – 8:00 PM MT
  • Weekly Commitment: 8–10 hours
    • Workshops
    • Mentorship sessions
    • Community-building activities
    • Project planning and execution

Eligibility Criteria

The Enlight Fellowship seeks passionate and driven individuals committed to improving youth mental wellbeing.

Who You Are

  • A leader working on solutions for youth mental health challenges
  • Committed to creating scalable and meaningful impact
  • Possess an entrepreneurial mindset and willingness to grow

Your Venture Stage

  • Operating for 1–5 years
  • Fewer than 10 employees
  • Demonstrated traction:
    • Active users or customers
    • Measurable results
    • Some funding (grants, revenue, or investments)

Commitment Requirements

  • Full participation in the 16-week program
  • Attendance at the mandatory in-person immersive experience
  • Active engagement in all virtual sessions and activities

Location

  • Open to applicants worldwide
  • Special focus on leaders addressing youth mental wellbeing in China and globally

Age Requirement

  • Applicants must be 18 years or older

Application Process

Step 1: Submit Application

  • Complete the online application (approximately 30 minutes)
  • Submit before the priority deadline for early consideration

Step 2: Application Review

  • Evaluated by the Admissions Committee
  • Focus on innovation, scalability, and impact

Step 3: Interview

  • Shortlisted candidates attend a 20-minute virtual interview

Step 4: Final Selection

  • Results announced by July 20, 2026
  • Selected fellows confirm participation
    APPLY HERE

Why You Should Apply for the Enlight Fellowship

This fellowship is an exceptional opportunity for social entrepreneurs who want to:

  • Scale their mental health initiatives globally
  • Gain access to world-class mentorship and training
  • Build a powerful international network
  • Strengthen their leadership and entrepreneurial skills
  • Create meaningful, sustainable change in their communities

The Enlight Fellowship goes beyond traditional programs by combining leadership development, real-world application, and global collaboration.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Enlight Fellowship 2026: Fully Funded Global Program for Youth Mental Wellbeing Leaders and Social Entrepreneurs appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Richard J. Margolis Award 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity for Nonfiction Journalists Driving Social Change
item.dc:creator: Dennis Mwangi
item.description:
The Richard J. Margolis Award stands as a distinguished recognition for emerging nonfiction writers dedicated to social-justice journalism. Designed to support developing journalists, this award provides both recognition and the invaluable opportunity to further meaningful storytelling that addresses critical societal issues. If you are a writer passionate about creating impactful nonfiction narratives and advancing public […]

The post Richard J. Margolis Award 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity for Nonfiction Journalists Driving Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Richard J. Margolis Award stands as a distinguished recognition for emerging nonfiction writers dedicated to social-justice journalism. Designed to support developing journalists, this award provides both recognition and the invaluable opportunity to further meaningful storytelling that addresses critical societal issues.

If you are a writer passionate about creating impactful nonfiction narratives and advancing public understanding of social values, this award could be a transformative step in your career.

About the Richard J. Margolis Award

The Richard J. Margolis Award celebrates excellence in nonfiction writing focused on social justice themes. It aims to empower writers who are committed to producing thoughtful, well-researched journalism that highlights important social concerns.

Each year, one recipient is selected and announced in November, followed by a residency at Blue Mountain Lake in the subsequent summer or fall. This residency provides a dedicated environment for writers to focus on their work and further develop their projects.

Application Timeline and Deadline

  • Applications are accepted year-round
  • To be considered for the current year’s award, submissions must be received by July 1
  • Applications submitted after July 1 will automatically be considered for the following year

What the Award Offers

Winning the Richard J. Margolis Award provides:

  • Recognition as an emerging voice in social-justice journalism
  • A prestigious writing residency at Blue Mountain Lake
  • Increased visibility within the literary and journalism communities
  • Opportunities for career advancement and networking

Application Requirements

Applicants must submit the following materials:

1. Cover Letter

  • Clearly state your interest in the award
  • Highlight your qualifications and experience

2. Project Description

  • Outline your current and upcoming work
  • Explain what you plan to develop during the Blue Mountain residency

3. Biographical Note

  • Provide a brief overview of your background, achievements, and writing journey

4. Writing Samples (2–3 pieces)

All samples must meet the following criteria:

  • Must be nonfiction only (no fiction or poetry)
  • Maximum of 30 pages total
  • At least one piece must not be memoir-based
  • Can be published or unpublished

Selection Criteria

The Selection Committee evaluates applications based on:

  • Quality of writing
  • Journalistic skill and clarity
  • Relevance to social justice themes
  • Potential impact of the proposed project

Applicants are encouraged to submit samples that demonstrate:

  • Strong storytelling ability
  • Investigative or analytical depth
  • A clear commitment to meaningful social issues

How to Submit Your Application

Preferred Method: Email Submission

  • Send all documents as PDF files
  • Use clear file naming starting with your last name
    • Example: Smith-coverletter.pdf, Smith-writingsample.pdf
  • Submit to: award@margolis.com

Alternative Method: Postal Mail

Send materials to:

Richard J. Margolis Award
c/o Margolis & Bloom
20 William Street, Suite 320
Wellesley, MA 02481

Note: Submitted materials will not be returned.

Responsibilities of Award Recipients

If selected, recipients are expected to:

  1. Acknowledge the award in:
    • Online and print biographies
    • Book acknowledgements
  2. Include a link to the official website in online profiles
  3. Provide updates on:
    • Publications
    • Career milestones
    • Honors and achievements

Updates can be sent via email or postal mail to the award committee.

Why You Should Apply

The Richard J. Margolis Award is more than just recognition—it is an opportunity to:

  • Advance your nonfiction writing career
  • Gain support for impact-driven journalism
  • Join a network of writers committed to social change
  • Dedicate time and space to a meaningful project

For writers striving to make a difference through storytelling, this award offers both credibility and creative freedom.

For more information about this opportunity, visit here.

Discover more Global Fellowship Opportunities on OFY here.

The post Richard J. Margolis Award 2026: A Prestigious Opportunity for Nonfiction Journalists Driving Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Future 100 Growth Fund Mentor Registration: Empowering the Next Generation of Black Founders in the UK
item.dc:creator: Stalin Daniel
item.description:
The Future 100 (F100) Growth Fund, a Sky-funded initiative, is a transformative programme designed to support Black founders across the United Kingdom. While the programme is widely recognized for providing financial backing and strategic growth opportunities to entrepreneurs, it also places strong emphasis on mentorship—an essential pillar for sustainable business success. To further strengthen its […]

The post Future 100 Growth Fund Mentor Registration: Empowering the Next Generation of Black Founders in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Future 100 (F100) Growth Fund, a Sky-funded initiative, is a transformative programme designed to support Black founders across the United Kingdom. While the programme is widely recognized for providing financial backing and strategic growth opportunities to entrepreneurs, it also places strong emphasis on mentorship—an essential pillar for sustainable business success.

To further strengthen its impact, the F100 Growth Fund is actively seeking experienced and passionate mentors who are ready to guide, inspire, and accelerate the journeys of emerging founders. If you have the expertise, network, and commitment to contribute meaningfully, this is a powerful opportunity to shape the future of entrepreneurship.

About the F100 Growth Fund Programme

The F100 Growth Fund is specifically tailored to uplift Black entrepreneurs by providing:

  • Funding support of up to £15,000 to help scale their businesses
  • Access to Sky’s supply chain, opening doors to large-scale business opportunities
  • Investor networking opportunities, enabling founders to connect with potential funders and partners

This initiative is not just about capital—it is about creating an ecosystem of growth, access, and long-term success.

Why Mentorship Matters in This Programme

Behind every successful entrepreneur is guidance, insight, and support. The F100 Growth Fund recognizes that mentorship can significantly accelerate business growth by:

  • Helping founders avoid common pitfalls
  • Offering strategic direction and decision-making support
  • Expanding access to industry networks
  • Providing real-world insights from experienced professionals

Mentors play a critical role in shaping confident, capable, and scalable businesses.

Who Should Apply as a Mentor?

The programme is looking for individuals who align with one or more of the following criteria:

1. Passion for Developing Future Entrepreneurs

  • A genuine commitment to nurturing and guiding the next generation
  • Enthusiasm for contributing to entrepreneurial growth and innovation

2. Willingness to Share Knowledge and Networks

  • Open to offering insights, advice, and connections
  • Interested in actively contributing to the mentee’s development

3. Experience in Technology or Digitally Enabled Businesses

  • Background in launching or scaling tech-driven ventures
  • Familiarity with digital transformation, platforms, or innovation

4. Fundraising Expertise

  • Proven experience in raising capital through:
    • Angel investors
    • Venture capital
    • Seed funding
    • Loan financing

This experience is invaluable in helping founders navigate funding challenges.

Mentor Commitment

The programme is designed to be impactful yet manageable for busy professionals. Mentors are expected to:

  • Commit at least 1 hour per month
  • Engage in meaningful conversations with founders
  • Provide actionable insights and guidance

This flexible commitment ensures that mentors can contribute effectively without overwhelming their schedules.

Benefits of Becoming an F100 Mentor

Participating as a mentor offers both personal and professional rewards:

  • Opportunity to shape emerging businesses
  • Expand your professional network within the entrepreneurial ecosystem
  • Gain exposure to innovative ideas and fresh perspectives
  • Contribute to diversity and inclusion in business
  • Enhance leadership and coaching skills

Mentorship is a two-way exchange—while founders gain guidance, mentors gain renewed perspectives and purpose.

How to Get Involved

If you meet the criteria and are ready to contribute your time and expertise, the F100 Growth Fund welcomes your application.

  • Ensure you can commit to at least one hour monthly
  • Reflect on how your experience aligns with the programme’s needs
  • Be prepared to actively support and guide founders

Deadline: Not specified (applications may be considered on a rolling basis—early application is recommended)

Final Thoughts

The Future 100 Growth Fund is more than a funding initiative—it is a movement toward equitable opportunity and long-term entrepreneurial success. By becoming a mentor, you are not just advising a founder; you are helping build the future of business leadership in the UK.

If you are ready to make a meaningful impact, this is your chance to step in and lead from the front.
For more information about this opportunity, click here.

Explore more global fellowship opportunities on OFY here.

The post Future 100 Growth Fund Mentor Registration: Empowering the Next Generation of Black Founders in the UK appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: IAU Mini-Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity to Empower Women and Girls in Astronomy and STEM
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The International Astronomical Union (IAU) is now accepting applications for its Mini-Grant program, supporting innovative, community-based projects that promote inclusion, leadership, and participation of women and girls in astronomy and STEM. This initiative is a valuable opportunity for organizations and individuals working to close gender gaps in science and create more inclusive pathways into astronomy […]

The post IAU Mini-Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity to Empower Women and Girls in Astronomy and STEM appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The International Astronomical Union (IAU) is now accepting applications for its Mini-Grant program, supporting innovative, community-based projects that promote inclusion, leadership, and participation of women and girls in astronomy and STEM.

This initiative is a valuable opportunity for organizations and individuals working to close gender gaps in science and create more inclusive pathways into astronomy and related fields.

About the IAU Mini-Grant

The IAU Mini-Grant is designed to fund impactful, grassroots projects that encourage women and girls to engage with astronomy and STEM disciplines. The program focuses on initiatives that build leadership skills, expand access, and foster long-term participation in scientific communities.

Projects should demonstrate creativity, community engagement, and a clear plan for impact.

What the Grant Supports

Selected proposals will receive funding to implement projects that:

  • Promote inclusion and diversity in astronomy and STEM
  • Empower women and girls through education and mentorship
  • Strengthen community-based science initiatives
  • Encourage leadership and participation in scientific fields

Why This Opportunity Matters

Despite growing interest in STEM, women and girls remain underrepresented in many scientific disciplines, including astronomy. This grant aims to address that gap by supporting initiatives that create opportunities, inspire participation, and build inclusive scientific communities.

Who Can Apply

The grant is open to applicants from the North American region who are working on projects aligned with the program’s goals.

Eligible applicants may include:

  • Nonprofit organizations and community groups
  • Educators and academic institutions
  • STEM advocates and project leaders
  • Individuals or teams leading community-based initiatives

Application Deadline

All applications must be submitted by:

May 31, 2026

Late submissions may not be considered, so applicants are encouraged to apply early.

How to Apply

Interested applicants can learn more and submit their proposals here.

For more Grant opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post IAU Mini-Grant 2026: Funding Opportunity to Empower Women and Girls in Astronomy and STEM appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Nova Fellows Program 2026 at the Belfer Center: A Unique Fellowship for Senior Tech and Science Innovators
item.dc:creator: Nana Anima Ampofo
item.description:
The Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs has launched an exciting new opportunity—the Nova Fellows Program, designed to bring senior technology and scientific innovators from the private sector into direct collaboration with policymakers, scholars, and students. Hosted at Harvard Kennedy School in Cambridge, this fellowship bridges the gap between cutting-edge innovation and global policy, […]

The post Nova Fellows Program 2026 at the Belfer Center: A Unique Fellowship for Senior Tech and Science Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Belfer Center for Science and International Affairs has launched an exciting new opportunity—the Nova Fellows Program, designed to bring senior technology and scientific innovators from the private sector into direct collaboration with policymakers, scholars, and students.

Hosted at Harvard Kennedy School in Cambridge, this fellowship bridges the gap between cutting-edge innovation and global policy, offering participants a platform to shape conversations around national security, diplomacy, and governance in an increasingly tech-driven world.

About the Nova Fellows Program

The Nova Fellows Program is part of the Belfer Center’s broader initiative focused on emerging technology, scientific advancement, and global policy. It aims to integrate real-world expertise from industry leaders into academic and policy discussions.

Selected fellows will join the Belfer Center on a part-time basis, contributing their knowledge while gaining new perspectives through collaboration with Harvard’s academic and policy community.

What Fellows Will Do

Participants in the program will:

  • Collaborate with Harvard faculty, researchers, and students
  • Work on research and policy-focused projects
  • Contribute to discussions on global challenges shaped by technology
  • Engage with a network of policymakers and international practitioners

This is an opportunity to not only share expertise but also influence how emerging technologies are understood and governed globally.

Why This Fellowship Matters

As advancements in AI, biotechnology, cybersecurity, and other fields continue to shape global systems, there is a growing need for collaboration between innovators and policymakers.

The Nova Fellows Program creates a space where:

  • Private-sector insights inform policy decisions
  • Academic research connects with real-world applications
  • Global challenges are addressed through interdisciplinary collaboration

Who Should Apply

This fellowship is best suited for:

  • Senior professionals in technology and scientific fields
  • Innovators from the private sector
  • Leaders working at the intersection of technology and global policy

Applicants should bring substantial experience and a strong interest in contributing to policy discussions and research initiatives.

Application Details

Applications are now open. While no fixed deadline has been specified, early application is strongly encouraged.

How to Apply

Learn more and apply here.

For more Fellowship Opportunities, Visit OFY here.

The post Nova Fellows Program 2026 at the Belfer Center: A Unique Fellowship for Senior Tech and Science Innovators appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Global South Scholars to Publish Groundbreaking Urban Research
item.dc:creator: Phionah Tumusiime
item.description:
The Urban Studies Foundation (USF) International Fellowships 2026 present a powerful opportunity for early-to-mid-career urban scholars from the Global South to advance their academic careers, expand global networks, and produce high-impact publications. Designed to strengthen urban research and amplify diverse global perspectives, this prestigious fellowship supports scholars in transforming completed research into publishable outputs while […]

The post Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Global South Scholars to Publish Groundbreaking Urban Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Urban Studies Foundation (USF) International Fellowships 2026 present a powerful opportunity for early-to-mid-career urban scholars from the Global South to advance their academic careers, expand global networks, and produce high-impact publications. Designed to strengthen urban research and amplify diverse global perspectives, this prestigious fellowship supports scholars in transforming completed research into publishable outputs while collaborating with leading institutions worldwide.

With funding for a dedicated sabbatical period, mentorship from experienced academics, and access to international research communities, this fellowship is a gateway to academic excellence and global influence in urban studies.

Overview of the USF International Fellowships 2026

The International Fellowship scheme is a flagship initiative by the Urban Studies Foundation aimed at:

  • Advancing global urban studies research
  • Supporting early-career researchers
  • Promoting knowledge exchange across borders
  • Enhancing research leadership in the Global South

Since its inception, the program has supported over 80 fellows from more than 20 countries, contributing significantly to the development of urban scholarship worldwide.

Fellowship Purpose and Focus

This fellowship is specifically designed to support scholars who have already completed their primary research and are ready to:

  • Write journal articles
  • Develop book chapters
  • Publish academic books

The program does not fund new research or data collection. Instead, it provides the time, resources, and environment needed to transform existing research into high-quality academic publications.

Research Areas Covered

Applicants may propose projects within any urban studies theme that contributes to understanding cities in the Global South. These include, but are not limited to:

  • Climate and sustainability
  • Urban development and governance
  • Housing and infrastructure
  • Migration and mobility
  • Public health and social equity
  • Urban planning and policy
  • Political economy
  • Transport and technology
  • Ecology and environment

Successful proposals should demonstrate a clear and original contribution to the field of urban studies, especially in relation to the unique development trajectories of cities in the Global South.

Mentorship and Host Institution

A key component of the fellowship is the collaboration with:

  • A host university or research institution (anywhere globally)
  • A senior academic mentor in the applicant’s field

Expectations:

  • The mentor provides academic guidance and support
  • The host institution offers a productive research environment
  • Fellows are integrated into research networks and communities
  • Long-term collaboration opportunities are encouraged

Fellowship Duration and Timeline

  • Duration: 3 to 9 months
  • Start period:
    • No earlier than 4 months after the deadline
    • No later than 14 months after the deadline

Funding and Benefits

The fellowship provides comprehensive financial support, including:

  • Accommodation and living expenses
  • Return international travel
  • Contribution toward research-related costs
  • Mentor engagement support (for collaboration with the fellow’s home institution or networks)

Additional Support:

Shortlisted candidates may apply for supplementary funding if they face:

  • Caring responsibilities
  • Disability-related costs

Eligibility Criteria

Applicants must meet the following requirements:

  1. Hold a PhD awarded within the last 10 years
  2. Be an early-to-mid-career urban scholar
  3. Be a national of an OECD DAC-listed ODA recipient country
  4. Be affiliated with a research institution in an eligible country
  5. Have completed primary data collection and analysis
  6. Be ready to produce publishable academic outputs

Application Requirements

Applicants must prepare and submit the following:

1. Applicant Information

  • Contact details
  • Academic background
  • Career statement
  • Proof of nationality
  • Two academic referees

2. Fellowship Proposal

  • Timeline and proposed dates
  • Host institution details
  • Mentorship arrangements

3. Budget Plan

  • Detailed cost breakdown
  • Supporting evidence for expenses above GBP 500

4. Research Proposal

  • Overview of research
  • Planned outputs
  • Justification for host institution
  • Role of mentor

5. Supporting Documents

  • Applicant CV (max 3 pages)
  • Mentor CV (max 3 pages)
  • Mentor support letter (max 2 pages)

Application Process

Click Here for more and to apply:

 Application Deadline

6th July 2026 at 23:59 (UTC+0)

Selection and Results

  • Decisions are typically made within 12 weeks after the deadline
  • All applicants will be notified of the outcome
  • No feedback will be provided on unsuccessful applications
  • All decisions are final

Why You Should Apply

This fellowship offers more than just funding—it provides a platform to:

  • Elevate your research to international standards
  • Collaborate with global academic leaders
  • Contribute to epistemic justice and inclusive knowledge production
  • Build long-term research partnerships
  • Strengthen your academic profile and leadership potential

For more global Fellowship opportunities click here.

The post Urban Studies Foundation International Fellowships 2026: Fully Funded Opportunity for Global South Scholars to Publish Groundbreaking Urban Research appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Senior Organiser Job (Worker-led Transition): Drive Climate Action and Worker Justice in 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Senior Organiser – Worker-led Transition role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of climate action, labour rights, and social justice. This 12-month position is designed for experienced organisers who want to help shape a fair transition to a low-carbon economy while supporting workers and industrial communities across the UK. About the […]

The post Senior Organiser Job (Worker-led Transition): Drive Climate Action and Worker Justice in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Senior Organiser – Worker-led Transition role offers a unique opportunity to work at the intersection of climate action, labour rights, and social justice. This 12-month position is designed for experienced organisers who want to help shape a fair transition to a low-carbon economy while supporting workers and industrial communities across the UK.

About the Program

This role is offered by New Economy Organisers Network in collaboration with Trades Union Congress as part of the Worker-led Transition project.

The initiative focuses on supporting workers in high-carbon industries—such as steel, automotive, and manufacturing—to actively shape a sustainable future for their sectors. It aims to ensure that climate transition efforts protect jobs, strengthen communities, and create new opportunities in clean industries.

The successful candidate will play a key role in building bridges between climate movements and trade unions, helping to align strategies and mobilise collective action across industrial regions.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As the UK transitions toward a decarbonised economy, hundreds of thousands of jobs are at risk without proper planning and investment. This role directly addresses that challenge by ensuring that workers are not left behind in the shift to greener industries.

By supporting a worker-led approach, the project promotes solutions that are both environmentally sustainable and socially just. It also contributes to countering divisive narratives by building a shared vision for economic and climate resilience in industrial communities.

For professionals committed to movement-building and systemic change, this role offers the chance to work on one of the most pressing issues of our time.

What You’ll Gain

This role provides hands-on experience in movement building, coalition development, and strategic organising at a national level.

You will develop and implement organising strategies, facilitate trainings, and work closely with community groups, activists, and trade union members. The position also offers the opportunity to co-design events and campaigns that bring together diverse stakeholders to drive change.

With a flexible 28-hour work week and a strong emphasis on work-life balance, the role supports both professional growth and wellbeing. You’ll also gain experience working within a collaborative, values-driven organisation focused on long-term impact.

Who Can Apply

This role is best suited to experienced organisers with at least three years of work in movement building, campaigning, or grassroots organising.

Ideal candidates will have experience in activities such as mapping and outreach, campaign design, political education, coalition building, and convening organisations around shared strategies. Strong facilitation and training skills are also essential, particularly in working with communities directly affected by injustice.

Applicants should be comfortable working in fast-moving environments, balancing strategic thinking with hands-on organising. Strong communication skills and the ability to build trust across diverse groups are key to success in this role.

An understanding of either the climate movement or the trade union movement is important, and experience working across both is particularly valuable.

The organisation is also seeking individuals committed to anti-oppression work and to building inclusive movements that challenge structural inequality in all forms.

Location and Conditions

The role is based in London, United Kingdom, with a hybrid working model requiring at least 25% in-office presence.

The position also involves regular travel across England and Wales (approximately 3–4 days per month), including occasional overnight stays.

This is a full-time role structured around a 28-hour work week, offering flexibility in how those hours are distributed.

Application Process and Deadline

Candidates must complete the application form and submit it along with an Equal Opportunities Monitoring form via email as outlined on the official job page.

The application deadline is May 10, 2026.

Learn more and apply on the official NEON website to be part of building a fair and worker-led climate transition.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Senior Organiser Job (Worker-led Transition): Drive Climate Action and Worker Justice in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships job offers a high-impact leadership opportunity for experienced professionals looking to shape national systems and scale economic opportunity initiatives. Based in New York (with an option for Washington, DC), this senior-level role focuses on building strategic partnerships, aligning funding, and translating local innovation into national impact. About the […]

The post Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships job offers a high-impact leadership opportunity for experienced professionals looking to shape national systems and scale economic opportunity initiatives. Based in New York (with an option for Washington, DC), this senior-level role focuses on building strategic partnerships, aligning funding, and translating local innovation into national impact.

About the Program

This role is offered by The Rockefeller Foundation, a globally recognized philanthropy committed to advancing human well-being through innovation in areas such as health, food systems, energy, and economic opportunity.

The Managing Director, National Partnerships plays a central role in advancing the Foundation’s U.S. Economic Opportunity and Civic Innovation agenda. Reporting to senior leadership, the role focuses on scaling proven solutions by connecting community-based insights with national platforms, policies, and funding strategies.

The position is highly external-facing and involves working closely with stakeholders across sectors—including philanthropy, government, financial institutions, and employers—to build partnerships that drive long-term, systemic change.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Addressing economic inequality and workforce challenges requires more than isolated interventions—it demands coordinated, large-scale solutions. This role is designed to bridge that gap by ensuring that successful local initiatives are translated into national systems and policies.

By aligning funding streams, influencing institutional practices, and fostering cross-sector collaboration, the Managing Director contributes to building sustainable pathways for economic mobility. The role also positions the successful candidate as a key influencer in shaping national conversations around workforce development and inclusive economic growth.

For professionals seeking to operate at the intersection of policy, finance, and social impact, this opportunity offers a platform to drive meaningful, large-scale change.

What Participants Will Gain

This position provides significant leadership experience at a national level, offering the opportunity to design and implement strategies that scale impact across multiple states and sectors.

The selected candidate will strengthen their expertise in partnership development, managing complex stakeholder ecosystems, and structuring funding strategies that combine philanthropic, public, and private capital.

The role also offers high-level exposure to policy development and institutional change, allowing professionals to influence how economic opportunity initiatives are designed and implemented across the United States.

In addition, working with the Rockefeller Foundation provides access to a global network of leaders, innovators, and institutions, enhancing both professional credibility and long-term career prospects.

Who Can Apply

This role is intended for senior professionals with extensive leadership experience. Applicants are generally expected to have at least 12 years of experience in fields such as philanthropy, government, finance, or economic development.

Candidates should demonstrate a strong track record of building and managing multi-stakeholder partnerships, as well as experience in scaling programs or initiatives to achieve broader impact.

A bachelor’s degree is required, while an advanced degree is preferred. Strong strategic thinking, leadership, and communication skills are essential, along with the ability to navigate complex systems and influence high-level stakeholders.

This position is based in New York City, United States, with an additional location option in Washington, DC. The role follows a hybrid work schedule, requiring in-office presence Monday through Thursday.

Application Process and Deadline

Interested candidates should apply through the official Rockefeller Foundation careers page, ensuring all required materials are submitted before the deadline.

The application deadline is April 29, 2026. Due to the senior nature of this role, applicants are encouraged to submit well-prepared applications highlighting leadership experience, partnership-building, and impact at scale.

Learn more and apply on the official Rockefeller Foundation website to be considered for this leadership role shaping national impact. When applying, highlight your experience scaling initiatives, managing partnerships, and influencing policy or funding systems.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Rockefeller Foundation Managing Director, National Partnerships Job: Lead Large-Scale Impact in 2026 appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7) offers a valuable opportunity for technology professionals to apply their skills within an international development context while contributing to impactful global initiatives. Based in Accra, this full-time position allows candidates to support digital operations and strengthen ICT systems within one of the United Nations’ leading agencies. About […]

The post UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7) offers a valuable opportunity for technology professionals to apply their skills within an international development context while contributing to impactful global initiatives. Based in Accra, this full-time position allows candidates to support digital operations and strengthen ICT systems within one of the United Nations’ leading agencies.

About the Program

The ICT Associate role is offered by United Nations Population Fund, the UN agency dedicated to ensuring that every pregnancy is intended, every childbirth is safe, and every young person can reach their full potential. Operating across multiple countries, UNFPA plays a key role in advancing global development goals, particularly in areas such as reproductive health, gender equality, and population data.

In Ghana, the ICT Associate will work within a dynamic country office environment, supporting the implementation of digital systems and infrastructure. The role is supervised by the International Operations Manager and involves overseeing ICT services, ensuring system reliability, and providing technical support to staff.

This is a fixed-term position with an initial duration of one year, with the possibility of extension based on performance and organizational needs.

Why This Opportunity Matters

As organizations increasingly rely on digital infrastructure, ICT professionals play a critical role in ensuring operational efficiency and service delivery. Within UNFPA, this role goes beyond technical support—it directly contributes to programs that improve lives and strengthen communities.

By maintaining secure and reliable systems, the ICT Associate enables teams to deliver essential services related to healthcare, gender equality, and development. This position therefore combines technical expertise with social impact, making it particularly relevant for professionals seeking purpose-driven careers.

Additionally, working within a UN agency provides exposure to international standards, cross-cultural collaboration, and complex operational environments, all of which are valuable for long-term career growth.

What Participants Will Gain

The ICT Associate will gain extensive experience managing and supporting enterprise-level ICT systems within a global organization. This includes working with infrastructure, software systems, and security protocols that support large-scale operations.

The role also offers the opportunity to develop leadership skills by overseeing ICT functions within the country office. Candidates will enhance their ability to troubleshoot technical issues, manage vendors, and implement digital strategies that improve organizational efficiency.

Beyond technical growth, the position provides exposure to international development work, allowing professionals to understand how technology supports global initiatives and policy implementation.

Working within UNFPA also enables candidates to build a strong professional network, connecting with experts across different sectors and countries.

Who Can Apply

This position is open to candidates with a bachelor’s degree in Information Technology, Computer Science, or a related field. Applicants should have at least three years of relevant professional experience in ICT roles within either the public or private sector.

Candidates should demonstrate strong technical knowledge, including familiarity with operating systems, network infrastructure, and ICT security systems. Experience with enterprise resource planning systems and corporate ICT environments is considered an advantage.

Strong analytical, problem-solving, and organizational skills are essential, along with the ability to manage multiple tasks and respond to technical challenges effectively.

Fluency in English is required, and knowledge of an additional UN language may strengthen an application.

Eligible Areas

This is a national position based in Accra, Ghana. Applicants must be eligible to work in Ghana.

Application Process and Deadline

The application deadline is May 9, 2026. Due to the competitive nature of UN roles, early applications are strongly encouraged.

Shortlisted candidates may be required to undergo additional assessments, including technical evaluations and interviews.

Learn more and apply on the official UNFPA job portal.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post UNFPA ICT Associate Job in Ghana (G7): Build a Career in Global Development and Technology appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.

item.title: Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change
item.dc:creator: April Bullard
item.description:
The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) Cambodia 2026 is a year-long leadership programme designed to empower young women with the skills, confidence, and networks needed to challenge gender inequality and create meaningful change in their communities. Supported by UN Women Cambodia, this initiative provides a safe and inclusive space for emerging leaders to develop transformative leadership […]

The post Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
item.content:encoded:
The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) Cambodia 2026 is a year-long leadership programme designed to empower young women with the skills, confidence, and networks needed to challenge gender inequality and create meaningful change in their communities. Supported by UN Women Cambodia, this initiative provides a safe and inclusive space for emerging leaders to develop transformative leadership and critical thinking abilities.

About the Program

The Youth Leadership Academy (YLA) is a unique programme co-created by Cambodian women leaders from diverse backgrounds who are passionate about advancing gender equality. It is rooted in lived experiences and designed to respond to the real challenges young women face in accessing leadership opportunities.

The programme runs over 8–10 months and combines four in-person workshops with mentorship and action-based learning. Participants engage in a structured journey that emphasizes personal growth, collaborative learning, and real-world application of feminist leadership principles.

Through this blended approach, participants are not only introduced to leadership concepts but are also supported in applying them in their daily lives, families, and communities. The programme prioritizes creating a safe, supportive environment where young women can express themselves, build confidence, and develop their voices.

Why This Opportunity Matters

Across many communities, young women—particularly those from marginalized backgrounds—face barriers to leadership development, networking opportunities, and access to platforms where their voices can be heard. The Youth Leadership Academy addresses these challenges by intentionally focusing on inclusion and equity.

By centering feminist leadership, the programme encourages participants to question societal norms, reflect critically on gender dynamics, and take action to address inequalities. This is particularly important in contexts where discussions around gender and social justice may be limited or inaccessible.

The programme also emphasizes community impact. Rather than focusing solely on individual development, YLA supports participants in becoming change-makers who can influence their environments and contribute to broader social transformation.

What Participants Will Gain

Participants in the Youth Leadership Academy gain a wide range of personal and professional benefits. They develop strong leadership and critical thinking skills that enable them to navigate complex social issues and advocate for change.

The programme helps participants build confidence and communication skills, empowering them to express their ideas clearly and engage in meaningful discussions. Through mentorship and peer learning, participants also expand their networks, connecting with like-minded individuals and experienced leaders.

A key component of the programme is action learning, where participants apply their knowledge to real-life situations. This hands-on approach strengthens their ability to translate ideas into practical solutions and initiatives within their communities.

Additionally, participants gain a deeper understanding of gender equality and feminist leadership, equipping them with the tools to challenge inequality and promote inclusive practices in various aspects of their lives.

Who Can Apply

The programme is open to young women aged 17 to 25 years old who are passionate about gender equality and social change. It is particularly aimed at individuals who have had limited access to leadership programmes or networks.

Young women from underrepresented groups, including those with disabilities or from ethnic minority communities, are strongly encouraged to apply. Applicants should demonstrate a genuine interest in issues affecting women and girls, as well as a willingness to engage in feminist leadership practices.

Participants must be available to fully commit to the programme over its duration, including attending all workshops and actively participating in mentorship and action learning activities.

Eligible Areas

This opportunity is specifically designed for young women based in Cambodia.

Application Process and Deadline

Applicants are required to complete an online application form before the deadline. The selection process focuses on identifying motivated individuals who show potential for leadership and a commitment to social impact.

The application deadline is April 30, 2026. Selected participants will be announced on May 8, 2026, followed by the programme’s inception workshop on May 17, 2026. The programme officially begins on May 22, 2026.

An online information session will also be held on April 18, 2026 to provide further details about the programme and application process.

Apply using the official Google Form application.

Explore More Opportunities for Youth

Want to discover more fellowships, scholarships, internships, and global programs like this one? Visit Opportunities for Youth to explore more opportunities.

The post Youth Leadership Academy Cambodia 2026: Build Feminist Leadership Skills and Drive Social Change appeared first on Opportunities for Youth.
